Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting significant renal impairment with possible nephrotic-range protein loss and impaired glucose handling. Mildly elevated ALT and low serum globulin are also noted. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity. Findings are consistent with acute or chronic kidney disease, possibly with diabetic nephropathy and poor glycemic control. Immediate clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.
